headline: Agent infrastructure techniques led the month while long-context training methods broke out as new entrants.
body: The top rotation was dominated by agent plumbing: orchestration, AI context management, scoped skills, Model Context Protocol, subagents, and cache hit rate all moved together, reflected in PAI's persistent-memory Life OS built on Claude Code and PostHog's pipeline that turns product signals into pull requests via background agents. A distinct cluster of long-context and distributed training techniques broke out as new entrants, including DeepSpeed Ulysses, Untied Ulysses, CPU offloading, and auto-scaling. On the model side, Gemma 4's 31B reached fourth on the open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU and on-device. Hallucination risk also surfaced concretely, with lawyers sanctioned for AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases.

cited_items.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
